_comment: Clinical decision pathway for Acute Otitis Media aligned to NHS Pharmacy First pediatric criteria.
label: Acute Otitis Media (Ear Infection)

questions.text: How long has the ear pain been present?
Is the child pulling at the ear or very irritable due to ear discomfort?
Is there reduced hearing in the affected ear?
Do you have any facial weakness or drooping?
Do you have swelling or redness behind the ear?
Has there been ear discharge from a known perforated eardrum?
Have symptoms clearly worsened despite 48-72 hours of self-care?

redFlags.description: Facial weakness with ear symptoms indicates possible serious complication
redFlags.message: Facial weakness with ear infection symptoms needs emergency assessment now.
Swelling behind the ear can indicate mastoiditis. Seek emergency care immediately.

redFlags.description: Neck stiffness or severe headache with ear symptoms
redFlags.message: Neck stiffness or severe headache needs urgent same-day review.

redFlags.description: Perforated eardrum with discharge requires urgent clinician assessment
redFlags.message: Ear discharge from a known perforation should be assessed urgently.

eligibilityRules.reason: Acute otitis media pharmacy pathway is restricted to ages 1-17

eligibilityRules.condition: q5 === true || q6 === true || q7 === true || q8 === true || q9 === true
eligibilityRules.reason: Red-flag or complicated ear infection features require non-pharmacy assessment

eligibilityRules.condition: age >= 1 && age <= 17 && q2 === true && (q3 === true || q4 === true || q10 === true) && q5 === false && q6 === false && q7 === false && q8 === false && q9 === false
eligibilityRules.reason: Meets pediatric acute otitis media Pharmacy First criteria

outcomeRules.reason: Adults are outside the Acute Otitis Media Pharmacy First pathway
Pediatric AOM criteria met for pharmacy treatment pathway

outcomeRules.condition: q2 === false && q3 === false && q4 === false
outcomeRules.reason: Symptoms do not currently indicate clear acute otitis media
Outside pharmacy criteria or exclusion factors present

selfCareAdvice: Use suitable pain relief and monitor symptoms. If symptoms worsen or do not improve, seek clinical review.
pharmacyTreatmentOptions: Amoxicillin 500mg three times daily for 5 days
Clarithromycin 500mg twice daily for 5 days (penicillin allergy)
safetyNetAdvice: Seek urgent help for neck stiffness, mastoid swelling, facial weakness, severe headache, or rapidly worsening illness.
pathwayPatientDisclaimer: Community pharmacists may assess and treat acute otitis media in eligible children and young people (age 1-17) under NHS Pharmacy First. Complicated features require urgent clinician assessment.
